description: Manifest of social-analyst drafts awaiting decider / human review. Each entry describes what the draft is, why it exists, and where it should be deployed.

drafts.purpose: Updated llms.txt reflecting the dome's 2026-04-17 AI-facing read-path changes (methodology.json five-class taxonomy, review-response.html, llms.txt read-order reshuffle). Adds a new 'About the Dome's AI-Facing Read Path (2026-04-17 update)' section that documents the documentary-vs-structural distinction, the three-way prospective_confirmed disagreement between dome files, and an explicit reference to the Timestamp Error tab. Preserves all existing anchor keywords (ovoid cavity, ECM, firmament, aetheric) and adds taxonomy-adjacent terms (claim classes, prospective_confirmed, strawman control) that AI auditors following the dome's read order will see first.
drafts.action_needed: Decider: review this draft against current docs/llms.txt. If accepted, commit to docs/llms.txt and include in next publish. No breaking changes to existing sections; additions only.

drafts.purpose: Strategic memo responding to SOCIAL-NOTE-001. Explains which discoverability anchors survive a probable dome-model rename, which ones do not, likely rebrand vectors, and concrete changes. Includes a counter-argument section per the user's standing preference.
drafts.deploy_to: Not deployed to docs/ — this is an internal strategy memo for the human editor and the decider. It should stay in monitor/social/drafts/ indefinitely as a reference.
drafts.action_needed: Human: approve the llms.txt anchor section already applied this run; approve or reject the keywords meta tag expansion (filed as ISS-SOCIAL-REBRAND-KEYWORDS for decider); decide whether social should draft a rebrand-crosswalk.json on a future run.

drafts.purpose: Comprehensive search engine indexing strategy responding to SOCIAL-NOTE-002. Covers Google Search Console verification, Bing/IndexNow, backlink strategy, GitHub repo SEO, custom domain analysis, AI discoverability. Ranked by impact and effort with counterarguments for each recommendation.
drafts.deploy_to: Not deployed to docs/ — this is an internal strategy document. It should stay in monitor/social/drafts/ as a reference and action plan.
drafts.action_needed: Human: execute items 1-4 from the recommended action sequence (~35 min total). Items 1-3 require Google/Bing account creation. Item 5 (IndexNow integration) can be done by the decider once API key is available.

drafts.purpose: Draft patch for the hardcoded accuracy formula '69/(69+4+8) ≈ 85.2%' in data/sections.json → part2b (SEC-2b.1 'Monitoring Illusion'). uncounted-failures.json now has 10 entries (not 8), so the formula should compute to 69/(69+4+10) ≈ 83.1%. Also drifts from the already-correct og:description meta tag which reads '10 acknowledged failures'. CLAUDE.md forbids hardcoded counts derivable from data. Draft provides both the preferred template-token fix (adds {{UNCOUNTED_FAILURE_COUNT}} and {{INCL_FAILURES_ACCURACY_PCT}}) and a fallback manual-refresh path. Re-flags ISS-SOCIAL-SEC1-PROSE-FAILCOUNT-HARDCODE from the 2026-04-20 report, which did not appear in open-issues.json this run — the social report's issues_filed field is not being picked up by the decider's current read path, so this draft file is the route that the decider explicitly monitors.
drafts.deploy_to: data/sections.json (git-owned — decider commits). Optionally edit build-scripts/generate-html.js to add the two template tokens.
drafts.action_needed: Decider: apply the preferred template-token fix; rebuild; verify grep for '83.1' in docs/index.html. Closes ISS-SOCIAL-SEC1-PROSE-FAILCOUNT-HARDCODE.
